headline: AI release velocity hit fever pitch this week — GPT-5.4 shipped, Gemini 3 dropped, and the frontier's getting weird

stories.title: OpenAI's GPT-5.4 & Thinking Mode: Basically the 'just get it done' model
stories.summary: OpenAI dropped GPT-5.4 Thinking on March 5, bringing 1M token context windows and native computer-use capabilities. Literally does spreadsheets, presentations, and multi-app workflows without hand-holding. Shut down GPT-5.1 models same week (March 11) and rolled out interactive learning for 70+ math/science topics.
stories.takeaway: Agentic workflows are lowkey here fr. The model plans its own thinking in real-time—you can adjust mid-response. This isn't just faster GPT-4, it's fundamentally different.

stories.title: Google's Gemini 3: The 'actually multimodal' upgrade arrives
stories.summary: Google shipped Gemini 3 Pro (March 12) and Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite (March 3) offering 2.5x faster response times at just $0.25/M input tokens. Flash-Lite hits 86.9% on GPQA Diamond (graduate-level reasoning). Introduces Deep Think reasoning mode for research and engineering challenges.
stories.takeaway: Tbh the pricing here is wild—frontier-level performance at basically commodity rates. Deep Think mode lets you dial reasoning depth per request instead of fixed models.

stories.title: Anthropic's DOD Lawsuit & Partner Network: The million-dollar bet
stories.summary: Anthropic sued the Pentagon (March 9) over supply-chain risk designation after refusing to allow mass surveillance and autonomous weapons use. Simultaneously announced $100M Claude Partner Network for enterprise adoption, with certification programs and co-investment for integrators.
stories.takeaway: This is the ethics-vs-money moment everyone's been waiting for. Anthropic's drawing hard lines on use cases while betting $100M that enterprises will pick them anyway. 30+ OpenAI/Google employees publicly backed them in court.

stories.title: Meta Acquires Moltbook, Bets $135B on AI Infrastructure
stories.summary: Meta snapped up Moltbook (AI agent social network) for its Superintelligence Labs (March 10), bringing creators of agentic frameworks in-house. Simultaneously planning $135B capex in 2026—nearly double last year—to bankroll data centers, custom silicon, and GPU clusters for the AI arms race.
stories.takeaway: Meta's literally betting the farm on AI agents. Cutting 16k jobs (20% of workforce) to fund infrastructure. The metaverse is dead; AI automation is the new play.

watchNext.title: Anthropic Institute Launch: Societal Impact Research Arm
watchNext.why: Anthropic launched a new research institute (March 11) focused on how AI reshapes jobs, economy, and society. Jack Clark leading as Head of Public Benefit. Worth watching bc this signals the biggest AI labs are finally doing the 'what about jobs' work mainstream media's been asking.

watchNext.title: Princeton's OpenClaw-RL: Making AI Agents Learn from Real Interactions
watchNext.why: Researchers dropped a framework turning live chat/terminal/GUI signals into training data. Just a few dozen interactions = noticeable improvements. This solves the 'agents throw away feedback' problem and lowers the barrier for custom agents fr.
